volumeTitle: Trojan Women. Iphigenia among the Taurians. Ion
label: Iphigenia among the Taurians (Section 1 - Section 190)

sections.label: Section 1
sections.original: Πέλοψ ὁ Ταντάλειος ἐς Πῖσαν μολὼν
sections.translation: Pelops , son of Tantalus , coming to Pisa with swift horses , married Oenomaus ’ daughter , and she gave birth to Atreus , whose children are Menelaus and Agamemnon ; from him I was born ,

sections.label: Section 5
sections.original: τῆς Τυνδαρείας θυγατρὸς Ἰφιγένεια παῖς ,
sections.translation: his child Iphigenia , by the daughter of Tyndareus . Where Euripus rolls about its whirlpools in the frequent winds and twists the darkening waves , my father sacrificed me to Artemis for Helen ’ s sake , or so he thought , in the famous clefts of Aulis .

sections.label: Section 10
sections.original: ἐνταῦθα γὰρ δὴ χιλίων ναῶν στόλον
sections.translation: For there lord Agamemnon mustered his expedition of a thousand ships of Hellas , wanting to take the crown of Troy in glorious victory and avenge the outrage to Helen ’ s marriage , doing this favor for Menelaus .

sections.label: Section 15
sections.original: δεινῆς δʼ ἀπλοίας πνευμάτων τε τυγχάνων ,
sections.translation: But when he met with dreadful winds that would not let him sail , he went to burnt sacrifices , and Calchas had this to say : Lord and general of Hellas , Agamemnon , you will not set free your ships from land until Artemis has your daughter Iphigenia

sections.label: Section 20
sections.original: λάβῃ σφαγεῖσαν · ὅ τι γὰρ ἐνιαυτὸς τέκοι
sections.translation: as a victim . For you once vowed to sacrifice to the torch-bearing goddess the most beautiful creature brought forth that year ; then your wife , Clytemnestra , bore a child in your house — ascribing the prize of beauty to me — whom you must sacrifice . And by the craft of Odysseus ,

sections.label: Section 25
sections.original: μητρὸς παρείλοντʼ ἐπὶ γάμοις Ἀχιλλέως .
sections.translation: they took me from my mother , pretending a marriage with Achilles . I came to Aulis ; held up high over the altar , I , the unhappy one , was about to die by the sword ; but Artemis gave the Achaeans a deer in exchange for me and stole me from them ; conducting me through the bright air ,

sections.label: Section 30
sections.original: πέμψασά μʼ ἐς τήνδʼ ᾤκισεν Ταύρων χθόνα ,
sections.translation: she settled me here in the land of the Taurians . A barbarian rules this land of barbarians : Thoas , who runs as quickly as the flight of birds , and so he received his name for his swiftness of foot . Artemis has made me the priestess in this temple .

sections.label: Section 35
sections.original: ὅθεν νόμοισι τοῖσιν ἥδεται θεὰ
sections.translation: Here I begin the rites , which the goddess delights in , of a banquet noble in name only — I am silent as to the rest , for I fear the goddess — for I sacrifice , by a custom of the city established earlier , any Hellene who comes to this land .

sections.label: Section 40
sections.original: κατάρχομαι μέν , σφάγια δʼ ἄλλοισιν μέλει
sections.translation: But others carry out the sacrifices , not to be spoken of , within the temple of the goddess .

sections.label: Section 42
sections.original: ἃ καινὰ δʼ ἥκει νὺξ φέρουσα φάσματα ,
sections.translation: But the strange visions which the night brought with it , I will tell to the air , if that is any relief . I dreamed that I had left this land

sections.label: Section 45
sections.original: οἰκεῖν ἐν Ἄργει , παρθένοισι δʼ ἐν μέσαις
sections.translation: to live in Argos , and to sleep in the midst of the maidens ’ rooms ; but the earth ’ s back was shaken by a tossing swell . When I escaped and stood outside , I saw the cornice of the house fall , and the whole roof hurled in ruins on the ground , from the highest pillars .

sections.label: Section 50
sections.original: μόνος λελεῖφθαι στῦλος εἷς ἔδοξέ μοι
sections.translation: One support of my father ’ s house was left , I thought , and it had yellow locks of hair waving from its capital , and took on human voice . In observance of the art of slaughtering strangers that I practice here , I gave it holy water as if it were about to die , while I wept .

sections.label: Section 55
sections.original: κλαίουσα . τοὔναρ δʼ ὧδε συμβάλλω τόδε ·
sections.translation: This is my interpretation of this dream : Orestes , whom I consecrated by my rites , is dead . For male children are the supports of the house ; and those whom I purify with holy water die . I cannot connect this dream to my friends ,

sections.label: Section 60
sections.original: Στροφίῳ γὰρ οὐκ ἦν παῖς , ὅτʼ ὠλλύμην ἐγώ .
sections.translation: for Strophius , when I perished , had no son . Now I wish to give libations to my brother , though he is absent from me — for I would be able to do this — with the attendants given me by the king , Hellene women . But why

sections.label: Section 65
sections.original: οὔπω τίνος πάρεισιν ; εἶμʼ ἔσω δόμων
sections.translation: are they not yet here ? I will go inside this temple of the goddess where I live . Exit Iphigenia .

sections.label: Section 67
sections.original: ὅρα , φυλάσσου μή τις ἐν στίβῳ βροτῶν .
sections.translation: ( entering cautiously .) Look out , take care that no one is in the path .

sections.label: Section 68
sections.original: ὁρῶ , σκοποῦμαι δʼ ὄμμα πανταχῆ στρέφων .
sections.translation: I am looking , and turning my eyes everywhere , in examination .

sections.label: Section 69
sections.original: Πυλάδη , δοκεῖ σοι μέλαθρα ταῦτʼ εἶναι θεᾶς
sections.translation: Pylades , do you think this is the hall of the goddess ,

sections.label: Section 70
sections.original: ἔνθʼ Ἀργόθεν ναῦν ποντίαν ἐστείλαμεν ;
sections.translation: for which we set sail from Argos ?

sections.label: Section 71
sections.original: ἔμοιγʼ , Ὀρέστα · σοὶ δὲ συνδοκεῖν χρεών .
sections.translation: Yes , Orestes ; and you must think so too .

sections.label: Section 72
sections.original: καὶ βωμός , Ἕλλην οὗ καταστάζει φόνος ;
sections.translation: And the altar , that drips with the slaughter of Hellenes ?

sections.label: Section 73
sections.original: ἐξ αἱμάτων γοῦν ξάνθʼ ἔχει τριχώματα .
sections.translation: Its dedications of hair , at least , are red with blood .

sections.label: Section 74
sections.original: θριγκοῖς δʼ ὑπʼ αὐτοῖς σκῦλʼ ὁρᾷς ἠρτημένα ;
sections.translation: Do you see the spoils hanging from the very walls ?

sections.label: Section 75
sections.original: τῶν κατθανόντων γʼ ἀκροθίνια ξένων .
sections.translation: Trophies of strangers that have been slain . But I must look all around and keep careful watch . [*]

sections.label: Section 77
sections.original: ὦ Φοῖβε , ποῖ μʼ αὖ τήνδʼ ἐς ἄρκυν ἤγαγες
sections.translation: O Phoebus , where have you again brought me into the snare , by your oracles , since I avenged my father ’ s blood by the murder of my mother , and was driven by successive Furies ,

sections.label: Section 80
sections.original: ἠλαυνόμεσθα φυγάδες ἔξεδροι χθονὸς
sections.translation: a fugitive , away from the land , and completed many winding courses ; and coming to you I asked how I might arrive at an end to whirling madness and my labors , which I have carried out , wandering all over Hellas . . . .

sections.label: Section 85
sections.original: σὺ δʼ εἶπας ἐλθεῖν Ταυρικῆς μʼ ὅρους χθονός ,
sections.translation: And you told me to go to the boundaries of the Tauric land , where Artemis , your sister , has an altar , and to take the statue of the goddess , which is said here to have fallen to this temple from heaven ; and , taking it by craft of some stroke of luck ,

sections.label: Section 90
sections.original: κίνδυνον ἐκπλήσαντʼ , Ἀθηναίων χθονὶ
sections.translation: to complete the venture by giving it to the Athenian land — what was to come next was not spoken of — and if I did this , I would have rest from my labors .

sections.label: Section 93
sections.original: ἥκω δὲ πεισθεὶς σοῖς λόγοισιν ἐνθάδε
sections.translation: I have come here , obedient to your words , to an unknown , inhospitable land . I ask you ,

sections.label: Section 95
sections.original: Πυλάδη — σὺ γάρ μοι τοῦδε συλλήπτωρ πόνου —
sections.translation: Pylades , for you are my accomplice in this task , what shall we do ? You see that the surrounding walls are high ; shall we ascend the steps leading up to the house ? But how might we escape notice ? Or loosening the bronze bars of the door with levers ,

sections.label: Section 100
sections.original: ὧν οὐδὲν ἴσμεν ; ἢν δʼ ἀνοίγοντες πύλας
sections.translation: of which we know nothing ? If we are caught opening the gates and contriving an entrance , we shall die . No , but before we die , let us escape on the ship in which we sailed here .

sections.label: Section 104
sections.original: φεύγειν μὲν οὐκ ἀνεκτὸν οὐδʼ εἰώθαμεν ,
sections.translation: Flight is not to be endured , nor are we accustomed to it ,

sections.label: Section 105
sections.original: τὸν τοῦ θεοῦ δὲ χρησμὸν οὐ κακιστέον ·
sections.translation: and we must not bring reproach on the god ’ s oracle ; let us leave the temple and hide in a cave which the black sea washes with its moisture — far from the ship , so that no one , seeing it , may tell the ruler , and then seize us by force .

sections.label: Section 110
sections.original: ὅταν δὲ νυκτὸς ὄμμα λυγαίας μόλῃ ,
sections.translation: But when the eye of gloomy night comes on , then we must dare to take the polished statue from the shrine , by any means . See where between the triglyphs there is an empty space to let ourselves down . The brave endure their labors ,

sections.label: Section 115
sections.original: τολμῶσι , δειλοὶ δʼ εἰσὶν οὐδὲν οὐδαμοῦ .
sections.translation: the cowardly are worth nothing at all .

sections.label: Section 116
sections.original: οὔ τοι μακρὸν μὲν ἤλθομεν κώπῃ πόρον ,
sections.translation: Yes , we did not come on a long sea voyage only to undertake a return home before the end ; but you have spoken wel , we must obey . We should go wherever we can hide and escape notice .

sections.label: Section 120
sections.original: οὐ γὰρ τὸ τοῦ θεοῦ γʼ αἴτιον γενήσεται
sections.translation: For it will not be the god ’ s fault if his sacred oracle falls to the ground without effect . We must endure . Exeunt Orestes and Pylades .

sections.label: Section 123
sections.original: εὐφαμεῖτʼ , ὦ
sections.translation: Keep a holy silence , you who inhabit the double clashing

sections.label: Section 125
sections.original: πέτρας Ἀξείνου ναίοντες .
sections.translation: rocks of the Black Sea !

sections.label: Section 126
sections.original: ὦ παῖ τᾶς Λατοῦς ,
sections.translation: O daughter of Leto , Dictynna of the mountains , to your hall , to the golden walls of your temple with beautiful pillars ,

sections.label: Section 130
sections.original: πόδα παρθένιον ὅσιον ὁσίας
sections.translation: I , the servant of the holy key-holder , bend my holy virgin steps . For I have left the towers and walls of Hellas , famous for horses , and

sections.label: Section 135
sections.original: ἐξαλλάξασʼ Εὐρώπαν ,
sections.translation: Europe with its forests , my father ’ s home .

sections.label: Section 137
sections.original: ἔμολον · τί νέον ; τίνα φροντίδʼ ἔχεις ;
sections.translation: I have come . What is the news ? What is troubling you ? Why have you brought me , brought me to the shrine , you who are the daughter of Atreus ’ son , master of a thousand ships and ten thousand soldiers ,

sections.label: Section 140
sections.original: ἐλθόντος κλεινᾷ σὺν κώπᾳ
sections.translation: who came to the towers of Troy with a famous fleet ?

sections.label: Section 143
sections.original: ἰὼ δμωαί ,
sections.translation: Oh ! My servants , how I am involved in mournful dirges ,

sections.label: Section 145
sections.original: ἔγκειμαι , τᾶς οὐκ εὐμούσου
sections.translation: in laments unfit for the lyre , of a song that is not melodious , alas ! alas ! wailing for my family . Ruin has come to me ; I am lamenting the life of my brother ,

sections.label: Section 150
sections.original: ζωᾶς , οἵαν οἵαν ἰδόμαν
sections.translation: such a vision I saw in my dreams , in the night whose darkness is now over .

sections.label: Section 152a
sections.original: ὀλόμαν ὀλόμαν ·
sections.translation: I am lost , lost ! My father ’ s house is no more ; alas for my vanished family ,

sections.label: Section 155
sections.original: φεῦ φεῦ τῶν Ἄργει μόχθων .
sections.translation: alas for the sufferings of Argos ! O fate , I had one brother only and you carry him off and send him to Hades . For him ,

sections.label: Section 160
sections.original: μέλλω κρατῆρά τε τὸν φθιμένων
sections.translation: I am about to pour over the back of the earth these libations and the bowl of the dead : streams of milk from mountain cows , and offerings of wine from Bacchus ,

sections.label: Section 165
sections.original: ξουθᾶν τε πόνημα μελισσᾶν ,
sections.translation: and the labor of the tawny bees ; these sacrifices are soothing to the dead .

sections.label: Section 168
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἔνδος μοι πάγχρυσον
sections.translation: ( To a servant ) Give me the golden vessel and the libation of Hades .

sections.label: Section 170
sections.original: ὦ κατὰ γαίας Ἀγαμεμνόνιον
sections.translation: O child of Agamemnon beneath the earth , I send these to you as one dead . Accept them ; for I will not bring to your tomb my yellow hair or my tears .

sections.label: Section 175
sections.original: τηλόσε γὰρ δὴ σᾶς ἀπενάσθην
sections.translation: I live far indeed from your country and mine , where I am thought to lie , unhappily slaughtered .

sections.label: Section 178
sections.original: ἀντιψάλμους ᾠδὰς ὕμνων τʼ
sections.translation: I will sing for you , my mistress , responsive songs and

sections.label: Section 180
sections.original: Ἀσιητᾶν σοι βάρβαρον ἀχὰν
sections.translation: a barbarian cry of Asian hymns ; this song , dear to the dead ,

sections.label: Section 185
sections.original: Ἅιδας ὑμνεῖ δίχα παιάνων .
sections.translation: Hades sings in laments , in chants — not songs of triumph .

sections.label: Section 186
sections.original: οἴμοι , τῶν Ἀτρειδᾶν οἴκων ·
sections.translation: Alas for the house of the Atreidae ; the light of their scepter , alas , of the ancestral house , is lost . Once they ruled

sections.label: Section 188
sections.original: πατρῴων οἴκων .
sections.translation: Tell me , what shall we do with the strangers ?

sections.label: Section 190
sections.original: βασιλέων ἀρχά ,
sections.translation: as prosperous kings in Argos , but troubles dart out from troubles : Pelops , on his horses swiftly whirling , made his cast ; the sun changed from its seat the holy beam of its rays .
